title: feat: Implement WebRTC screen mirroring for Tesla Model 3 Highland
body:
## Summary

This PR implements the core teslaScreenFlow app - an Android application that streams the phone screen and system audio to a Tesla Model 3 Highland center display via WebRTC over local Wi-Fi.

## Features

- Screen Capture: MediaProjection foreground service capturing 1920x1080 @ 60fps
- Audio Capture: AudioPlaybackCaptureConfiguration for system audio (OPUS)
- WebRTC Streaming: H.264 4Mbps hardware encoding, STUN-based NAT traversal
- Web Server: NanoHTTPD on port 8080 hosting the frontend page
- Signaling: Java-WebSocket on port 8081 for SDP/ICE exchange
- Touch Feedback: AccessibilityService dispatches touch gestures from car browser back to phone
- Multi-client Ready: Signaling layer supports multiple clients (rear screen expansion)
- CI/CD: GitHub Actions workflow for build and test

## Architecture

Android Phone (Offerer) <-> WebRTC (H.264 + OPUS) <-> Tesla Browser (Answerer)

## Files

24 files, 1917 lines of code including complete README with install guide and FAQ.

Generated with Claude Code
head: feature/webrtc-tesla-screenflow
